title: Link Github to Puhti
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a07o3","text":"How to connect your github account to a local machine or an HPC cluster (example at Puhti CSC)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ai13e","text":"You'll need either a local or remote machine where Git is installed (https://git-scm.com/downloads) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":69,"length":29}],"data":{}},{"key":"9dbri","text":"You'll need a github account (https://github.com/)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":30,"length":19}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"68CF1C54C8F411EBA7DA0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://git-scm.com/downloads"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"7927D9EEC8F411EBBA610A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://github.com"}}}}
steps: Open a terminal and log onto the remote machine (if applicable). In order to quickly test that Git is installed on the machine type: git --help You should have the git usage printed out!

To configure the Git account type: git config --global user.name "Your name here" git config --global user.email "your_email@example.com" Replace the above code with your github username and email address.

Next we'll set up the SSH keys between Github and the machine. Check if you already have ssh keys on the machine. SSH keys are typically stored in your home directory under a .ssh/ folder in files named id_rsa and id_rsa .pub cd ~/.ssh/ ls Do you see any id_rsa and id_rsa.pub files?
Then log into your Github account and open your profile settings.
In settings, navigate to SSH and GPG Keys
Finally, create a new SSH key, give it a name and paste the content of the id_rsa.pub file. Save the new key.
Open a new terminal window and connect to the remote machine (if applicable). Check that everything was set up properly by typing: ssh -T git@github.com You should get the following message:
Next, connect to your Github account and go to your profile's settings
In Settings, navigate to SSH and GPG Keys section
Finally create a new SSH key, give it an appropriate name and paste the content of the id_rsa.pub file. Save the key and close Github!

Finally we can check if everything went well! Open a new terminal window and connect to the remote machine (if applicable). Test the configuration by typing: ssh -T git@github.com you should see the following message:
creator: Alise J Ponsero
